data.title: Two South Korean Police Officers Indicted for Aiding $186 Million Crypto Laundering Network
data.description:
South Korean prosecutors have indicted two police officers for allegedly taking bribes to help a criminal organization launder $186 million through illegal cryptocurrency exchange operations. Both officers have been suspended as the investigation expands, marking one of the most serious corruption cases tied to Korea’s growing crypto-crime landscape.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/two-south-korean-police-officers-indicted-for-aiding-186-million-crypto-laundering-network/

data.title: Bitcoin Hits a Cycle-Defining Support Zone as Whales Face Deepest Losses Since 2022
data.description:
Bitcoin has reached one of the most critical technical levels of the entire cycle, with several major indicators converging at the same moment. After 2. 5 years of climbing within a rising parallel channel, the price is now retesting the channel’s base, a point historically associated with decisive reversals or brutal breakdowns

data.title: Ethereum Price Surges 17% as ETF Inflows Hit $291M in Four Days — Here is What’s Fueling the Rebound
data.description:
The price of Ethereum has experienced a remarkable surge of 17% recently, as a significant inflow of capital into exchange-traded funds (ETFs) reached an impressive total of $291 million within just four days. This substantial increase in investment is driving a notable rebound in the cryptocurrency market, and various factors are contributing to this upward momentum. In this article, we will explore the key elements and dynamics that are fueling this resurgence in Ethereum's value and what it may mean for the broader financial landscape.
source: https://blocknews.com/ethereum-price-surges-17-as-etf-inflows-hit-291m-in-four-days-here-is-whats-fueling-the-rebound/

data.title: The UniSat UTXO tool now offers a fee model with rates less than 1 SAT/VB, improving idle asset utilization and optimizing transaction costs.
data.description: On November 28th, UniSat, a Bitcoin ecosystem service provider, announced the official launch of a "less than 1 sat/vB" fee mode in its UTXO management tool. This feature allows users to set a custom fee rate of less than 1 sat/vB (0.1-0.9 sat/vB) when network conditions are suitable, converting idle UTXOs into usable Bitcoin and reducing capital recovery costs. The tool automatically categorizes scattered UTXOs generated by BRC-20 minting and transfer operations and provides a one-click merging and recovery function. For example, a Native SegWit address containing 500 UTXOs (546 sats each) achieves a 98.7% capital recovery rate using a 0.1 sat/vB fee rate, approximately 11.7 percentage points higher than the standard 1 sat/vB rate. In addition to the asset recovery function, the tool's standardized operations such as batch sending, splitting, unlocking, locking, and merging also support the low-fee mode. The platform also notes that low-rate transactions may experience longer confirmation times or even fail to be confirmed during periods of network congestion. Users are advised to adjust their rate strategies flexibly based on real-time network conditions.
